content: Bob Marley was born on 6 February 1945 in Jamaica. He is closely tied to reggae culture and cannabis symbolism. Major songs commonly cited include No Woman, No Cry, One Love, Redemption Song, Could You Be Loved, Three Little Birds, Jamming, and Buffalo Soldier.

Snoop Dogg is widely known for openly discussing cannabis use as part of his public image. Other public figures commonly associated with openly pro-cannabis identities include Willie Nelson, Wiz Khalifa, B-Real, Seth Rogen, and Tommy Chong.

Reggae culture is strongly associated with Jamaica, Rastafari influence, spiritual and social commentary, bass-heavy rhythm, anti-oppression themes, communal identity, and cannabis symbolism. Colors commonly associated with reggae and Rastafari are red, gold, green, and often black.
The number 420 is a widely recognized cannabis-culture reference used in media, events, and casual slang.

Cannabis legality and cannabis culture are different things. A country can have strong cannabis culture while still keeping strict legal penalties, and a legal market can exist without a major cultural identity around cannabis.
